name: Persistent Pupillary Membranes (PPM)
description: PPMs are remnants of foetal blood vessels crossing the pupil that fail to fully regress after birth. Depending on what they attach to, they can cause iris-to-iris strands (benign), iris-to-lens adhesions (cataracts), or iris-to-cornea adhesions (corneal opacities).
treatment_overview: Iris-to-iris strands require no treatment. Iris-to-cornea or iris-to-lens forms may require monitoring or surgical management if causing significant visual impairment.
prevention_tips: Do not breed dogs with significant iris-to-cornea or iris-to-lens PPMs
